title: malva × adulterina
intro: Malva × adulterina, also known as the hollyhock mullein or hollyhock mallow, is a sterile hybrid plant derived from the combination of the hollyhock (Alcea rosea) and the mallow (Malva sylvestris). The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Malva × adulterina include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
